Nurse Emily needs to prepare a total of 3 writing prompts per group, and there are 4 groups, so the initial prompts will be $ 4 \times 3 = 12 $ prompts. She also has an additional set of 10 prompts to be shared equally among all the groups, which means each group gets $ \frac{10}{4} = 2.5 $ prompts. However, since the number of prompts must be a whole number, Emily will need to prepare 10 extra prompts in total. 

In addition to the initial 12 prompts, Emily will need to provide 10 extra prompts. Therefore, the total number of writing prompts Emily needs to prepare is $ 12 + 10 = 22 $.

The library contains a variety of writing prompts that can be used to support the residents, and the library also offers a selection of creative writing resources that can be shared among the groups. The library is a valuable resource for Emily to draw from when preparing the prompts for the session.

The END.
